days.title: Solo — Tiong Bahru, Sim Lim, Spectra
days.summary: Foodie morning in Tiong Bahru, hardware hunting at Sim Lim, Led Zeppelin tribute and Spectra at night.

days.title: Kopitiam morning · wife arrives LH780
days.summary: Solo morning at Tong Ah + Keong Saik, then T2 pickup at 4:30pm, easy evening near the hotel.

days.title: Fort Canning · Civic District · CÉ LA VI
days.summary: Together for the first full day — park, National Museum, Esplanade, and the MBS rooftop at 7:30pm.

days.title: Gardens by the Bay — the big one
days.summary: Ya Kun + Sultan Mosque morning, Flower Dome to Garden Rhapsody in the afternoon and evening.

days.title: Little India + Peranakan dinner
days.summary: Tekka, Mustafa, long siesta, then True Blue Cuisine at Armenian St.

days.title: Chinatown · bumboat · crab
days.summary: Temples, Maxwell, Telok Ayer walk, river cruise at sunset, Jumbo Seafood.

days.title: Katong + Jewel sendoff
days.summary: Slow Katong morning, 328 laksa, Jewel Canopy Park, flight home.

places.name: Hotel Waterloo Singapore
places.description: Your base for the week — 78 Waterloo St, recently rebranded Handwritten Collection. Central, walkable to Bugis, Kampong Glam, Bras Basah, Civic District.
tips: Request a late checkout for Fri 22 May.
Bag storage available after checkout.

places.name: Tiong Bahru Bakery & Market
places.description: Kouign-amann and flat white at the bakery (56 Eng Hoon St), then upstairs to the 1950s art deco hawker for chwee kueh, lor mee, Loo's Hainanese curry rice.
places.pregnancyNotes: All cooked. Chwee kueh and curry rice are safe.

places.name: Tiong Bahru street walk
places.description: Yong Siak St and Eng Hoon St — murals, indie bookshops (Books Actually), 1930s walk-up flats.

places.name: Sim Lim Square
places.description: Six floors of hardware, components, and used gear. Cash gets better prices. Ground floor for phones and cameras; upper floors for components.
tips: Cash only at most stalls.
Upper floors are less touristy — better deals on components.

places.name: Lau Pa Sat & Satay Street
places.description: Victorian cast-iron market. Walk the indoor stalls, then Satay Street (Boon Tat St) — grills fire up at 7pm, stalls 7 & 8 are the famous ones.
places.pregnancyNotes: Chicken and beef satay, nasi lemak, sugar cane juice — all well-cooked.

places.name: Led Zeppelin tribute — Immigrant Sonz
places.description: Immigrant Sonz at the DBS Foundation Outdoor Theatre (Esplanade outdoor stage). Free, grass seating.
tips: Bring a small towel to sit on the grass.

places.name: Spectra at Marina Bay
places.description: Free light and water show off MBS Event Plaza. 9pm nightly. Walk across Helix Bridge to get there after the concert.
tips: Watch from the boardwalk in front of the Merlion for the best reflection.

places.name: Tong Ah Eating House
places.description: Heritage kopitiam in a 1930s shophouse at 35 Keong Saik Rd. Kaya toast, kopi-o, Hainanese pork chop noodles.
places.pregnancyNotes: Hard-boiled eggs, not half-boiled. Cap kopi at 2/day.

places.name: Keong Saik & Bukit Pasoh walk
places.description: Singapore's prettiest restored shophouses — Keong Saik St and Bukit Pasoh Rd. Quiet on Sunday mornings.

places.name: Hong Lim Food Centre
places.description: Off-the-tourist-track hawker at 531A Upper Cross St. Ya Hua Bak Kut Teh nearby; inside: Ji Ji Wanton Mee, economy rice stalls.
places.pregnancyNotes: Bak kut teh is fully cooked. Wanton mee is fine.

places.name: Changi T2 — wife arrives LH780
places.description: Track LH780 via FlightAware or the Changi Airport app. Wait near Arrivals Hall A (Starbucks is right there). Flight lands 16:30; she should clear by 17:45–18:00.
tips: Tanah Merah is the interchange — take the Changi Airport branch there.
Grab home (~SGD 25, 30 min) is justified: jet-lagged + luggage + heat.

places.name: Albert Centre Market & Food Centre
places.description: 5-minute walk from the hotel. Bak kut teh, soon kueh, tau suan dessert — easy dinner for a jet-lagged arrival evening.
places.pregnancyNotes: Bak kut teh is peppery pork rib soup, fully cooked. All recommended dishes here are fully cooked.
tips: Use a tissue packet to reserve a seat — local custom.

places.name: Kampong Glam — Sultan Mosque at night
places.description: Gentle post-dinner stroll. Sultan Mosque lit beautifully at night, then murals and lanterns along Arab Street and Haji Lane.
tips: Stop at any Haji Lane café for a mocktail or fresh juice.

places.name: Toast Box, Bugis Junction
places.description: Classic kaya toast set, kopi, 8 min walk from the hotel.
places.pregnancyNotes: Skip the half-boiled eggs — ask for fully cooked. Cap kopi/teh at ~2 cups/day.

places.name: Fort Canning Park
places.description: Tree Tunnel (the spiral staircase photo), Spice Garden, Sundial Garden, ASEAN Sculpture Park. Flat shaded paths.

places.name: National Museum of Singapore
places.description: Heavily AC. The History Gallery gives great context for the week. 5 min walk downhill from Fort Canning.
tips: Great rainy-day backup if today's plan shifts.

places.name: Funan Mall food court
places.description: AC food court at Funan Mall (107 North Bridge Rd) — varied, light, good AC rest stop between morning and evening.
tips: There's also a supermarket if you want to stock up on snacks.

places.name: Civic District stroll
places.description: The Padang, St Andrew's Cathedral, and the Esplanade Park waterfront. The whole area lights up beautifully at dusk.

places.name: Esplanade Roof Garden
places.description: Free. Take the lift up Esplanade Mall. Sweeping view of Marina Bay at golden hour — much less crowded than MBS.
tips: Plan to be here by 6:30pm for golden hour.

places.name: Helix Bridge walk
places.description: Walk across the lit DNA-helix structure to MBS. Scenic at dusk — short crossing.

places.name: CÉ LA VI SkyBar
places.description: 57th floor at Marina Bay Sands Tower 3. SGD 35 cover fully redeemable against drinks. Virgin cocktail menu. Spectra light show patterns visible from above at 9pm.
tips: Dress code: covered shoes, sleeved shirt, long pants.
Arrive by 7:30 to secure a window seat.

reservation.notes: SGD 35pp cover charge fully redeemable against food and drinks. Mon 18 May 7:30pm.

places.name: Ya Kun Kaya Toast, Bugis Junction
places.description: Heritage chain, kaya toast set. 8 min walk from the hotel.
places.pregnancyNotes: Hard-boiled eggs, not the half-boiled default.

places.name: Sultan Mosque & Arab Street by day
places.description: Sultan Mosque by day — you can enter the main prayer hall. Malay Heritage Centre courtyard next door. Fabric shops and Haji Lane murals around the corner.

places.name: Zam Zam
places.description: Open since 1908. Legendary murtabak — chicken or mutton stuffed flatbread, griddled to order. 697-699 North Bridge Rd.
places.pregnancyNotes: Murtabak filling is fully cooked. Safe.

places.name: Flower Dome (Gardens by the Bay)
places.description: Cool, AC, new seasonal floral display. Combo ticket (~SGD 43 each). First stop — MRT Downtown Line: Bencoolen → Bayfront, then walk 5 min through the underpass.

reservation.notes: Combo ticket: Flower Dome + Cloud Forest + Supertree Observatory. Book on Klook or official site. Aim for 3:30pm entry.

places.name: Cloud Forest
places.description: 35m indoor waterfall, AC, Jurassic World animatronics. Lifts available throughout. Second stop after Flower Dome.
tips: Take an AC rest break on the benches between domes.

places.name: Supertree Grove & Observatory
places.description: Observatory at sunset (SGD 14 each). After dinner, stay on the grass at OCBC Garden Plaza for Garden Rhapsody at 8:45pm — free synchronised light and music show.
tips: Garden Rhapsody runs nightly — no booking needed.

places.name: Satay by the Bay
places.description: Hawker centre inside the Gardens. Chicken satay, Hainanese chicken rice, sugar cane juice — casual, well-cooked, perfect before Garden Rhapsody.
places.pregnancyNotes: Satay is well-grilled, safe. Skip any oyster omelette or rojak stalls.

places.name: Chye Seng Huat Hardware
places.description: Industrial-style café in Jalan Besar, 15 min walk from hotel. Proper Western-style brunch and good coffee for a change of pace.
tips: Closed Mondays — Wednesday is fine.

places.name: Sri Veeramakaliamman Temple
places.description: One of Singapore's oldest Hindu temples, dedicated to Kali. 141 Serangoon Rd — 5 min walk from Tekka.

places.name: House of Tan Teng Niah
places.description: The rainbow shophouse — your Little India photo stop. 37 Kerbau Rd.
tips: Pair with Sri Veeramakaliamman + Tekka in one loop.

places.name: Tekka Centre
places.description: Wet market downstairs, food court upstairs. South Indian thosai, biryani, roti prata with chicken curry. Skip rojak. Order a mango lassi.
places.pregnancyNotes: Skip the rojak (raw veg). Mango lassi over kopi if you've hit your caffeine cap.

places.name: Mustafa Centre
places.description: 24/7 four-floor everything store — gifts, snacks, electronics, toiletries, anything you forgot. Good prices on basics.
tips: Best for spices, snacks, and anything you forgot to pack.

places.name: Peranakan Museum
places.description: Optional 30–40 min visit before dinner. 39 Armenian St — 1 min walk to True Blue. SGD 10 entry.
tips: Skip if tired — True Blue itself is in a heritage shophouse with full Peranakan context.

places.name: True Blue Cuisine
places.description: Heritage Peranakan at 47/49 Armenian St. Michelin Bib Gourmand. Order Ayam Buah Keluak, Beef Rendang, Itek Sio, Ngoh Hiang. 8 min walk home.
places.pregnancyNotes: All dishes are fully cooked slow-braises. Safe.

reservation.notes: Book on Chope or call +65 6440 0449. Aim for 7pm Wed 20 May.

places.name: Ya Kun Kaya Toast, Bugis Junction
places.description: Kaya toast, hard-boiled eggs — clean start before Chinatown. 8 min walk from hotel.

places.name: Sri Mariamman Temple
places.description: Singapore's oldest Hindu temple — the colourful gopuram on South Bridge Road. 244 South Bridge Rd.
tips: Shoes off at the entrance.

places.name: Buddha Tooth Relic Temple
places.description: Striking red-and-gold temple with a free rooftop garden. Slow walk through Pagoda St and Smith St to get here.
tips: Rooftop garden is a cool, quiet breather.

places.name: Maxwell Food Centre
places.description: Pregnancy-friendly hit list: Ah Tai Hainanese Chicken Rice (#01-07), Marina South fried carrot cake (cooked egg), Lao Ban soya beancurd for dessert.
places.pregnancyNotes: Ah Tai's chicken is poached then chilled — fully cooked. Skip oyster omelette and rojak stalls.

places.name: Telok Ayer & Ann Siang Hill
places.description: Thian Hock Keng temple on Telok Ayer St, then up Ann Siang Rd through restored shophouses and café-lined lanes. Stop for an iced drink.

places.name: Singapore River Cruise (Clarke Quay)
places.description: 40-min bumboat ride from Clarke Quay jetty at sunset. Passes Cavenagh Bridge, Merlion, Fullerton, MBS. Seated and scenic.

reservation.notes: Book on Klook for the 6:30pm slot.

places.name: Jumbo Seafood, Riverside Point
places.description: Where the cruise drops you. Chilli crab and black pepper crab — fully cooked. The must-eat.
places.pregnancyNotes: Skip the cockles and clam sides. Crab is fine.

places.name: Chin Mee Chin Confectionery
places.description: Legendary kaya toast in Katong — charcoal-toasted bread, kaya by the spoon. MRT ~30 min from hotel.

places.name: Koon Seng Road shophouses
places.description: The pastel Peranakan shophouse row — your photo stop. Walk down Joo Chiat Rd for tile murals; browse Rumah Bebe.
tips: Best light before 11am.

places.name: 328 Katong Laksa
places.description: THE laksa. Noodles cut short so you eat with a spoon. Add an otah on the side. 51 East Coast Rd.
places.pregnancyNotes: Order "no cockles, please" — prawns and fishcake are fully cooked and fine.

places.name: Jewel — Rain Vortex & Forest Valley
places.description: Walk under the Rain Vortex (free, runs hourly). Shiseido Forest Valley indoor garden. Store luggage at T1 or T3 left-luggage (~SGD 14/bag) before heading here.
tips: Left-luggage counters open from mid-morning — store bags before Jewel.
Jewel is connected to T1, T2, T3 by walkway or Skytrain.

places.name: Jewel Canopy Park
places.description: Level 5 of Jewel. Mirror Maze + Hedge Maze. Skip the bouncing nets.

places.name: Jewel B2 dinner
places.description: B2 food options: Tim Ho Wan (dim sum), Five Star Chicken Rice, or Burger & Lobster. Rain Vortex light show at 9pm — walk back for the final send-off before your terminal.
places.pregnancyNotes: Tim Ho Wan's dim sum is fully cooked. Chicken rice is fine.

places.name: Changi Airport (departure)
places.description: Near-midnight flight home. Changi immigration is famously fast — 5 min even at peak.
tips: Connected to Jewel by walkway or Skytrain — no Grab needed once at the airport.
